strategy: Accuracy first. Any fizzle gets the fizzler a 0-pip Skeletal Dragon (800+1998 Death over 3 rounds), so enchant hits with accuracy and do not gamble low-accuracy spells.
Ervin keeps a -90% Tower Shield he recasts whenever damaged as long as a minion lives. Kill the minions first, or use Steal Ward or a pierce hit to get through.

Set up safely: single traps make him Cleanse Ward, so use Indemnity, Vaporize, or multi-trap spells like Windstorm (which do not trigger it). Any charm makes Taurus Disarm you and hand Ervin a Deathblade, so Aegis-protect blades or Dissipate the Disarm.
Quench Taurus's Smoke Screen (-50%) every third round; it sets up Ervin's Skeletal Dragon.
Clear the minions, then blade up and hit Ervin with Ice or Life (+35%, resists Death 80%). After minions die he throws a 0-pip Leviathan every other round, so do not stall.
cheatNotes: He wears a -90% Tower Shield and recasts it whenever damaged while a minion is alive, so kill the minions or you fight through the shield every hit.
Steal Ward or a pierce hit removes the shield without the damage-recast, though he still replaces it once he takes damage again.
Every fizzle earns the fizzler a 0-pip Skeletal Dragon for 800+1998 Death over 3 rounds, so stack accuracy and avoid low-accuracy hits.
Once the minions are gone he throws a 0-pip Leviathan (2030 Storm) at the highest-threat wizard every second round, so finish quickly.
That Leviathan can be dispelled with Dissipate even though it is a cheat-cast.
A single trap on Ervin makes him Cleanse Ward it off while any minion lives; use Indemnity or a multi-trap spell instead.
Vaporize dispels the Cleanse Ward cheat.
Multi-trap spells like Windstorm or Mass Death Prism do not trigger the Cleanse Ward, so use them to trap him safely.
Indemnity on your trap avoids the Cleanse Ward entirely.
Taurus drops a -50% Smoke Screen every third round to set up Ervin's hits; Quench it to keep your accuracy.
Any charm you cast makes Taurus Disarm the recipient and hand Ervin a free Deathblade; Aegis your blades to protect them.
Dissipate stops this, and dispelling the Disarm also denies Ervin the Deathblade.
Even Plague or Smoke Screen count as charms here and get you Disarmed, so watch what you cast.
Aegis on the charm avoids Taurus's Disarm.
Shadow-creature charms like Shadowblade also trigger the Disarm.
